summary: A small two-story rectangular house with brick walls, a dark stepped/gabled roof, horizontal wooden band between floors, stone corner accents, and a raised white stone (quartz-like) border platform around the base. Repeated narrow windows run along both floor levels on the visible facades.

shape.overall_form: compact rectangular block with a symmetrical stepped dark roof centered along the long axis
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long centerline (mirrored front/back and left/right appear symmetric)

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: corner/vertical accents (but appears as chiseled/regular stone variants)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: white border/trim (likely quartz or smooth stone slabs)

elements: rows of narrow windows on both first and second floors
stone-like vertical corner pillars at short edges
dark stepped/gabled roof with white slab trim along eaves
raised surrounding white slab platform (border) around foundation
rebuild_hints: Footprint: rectangular; walls two floors tall of brick with a one-block high wood plank strip wrapping horizontally between floors.
Place stone/stonebrick corner pillars at each short-edge corner rising full building height; inset continuous rows of 1x1 glass panes spaced evenly across facades.
Roof: use nether_brick blocks to create a symmetrical stepped gable roof peaked at the centerline; top edges trimmed with white slabs at eaves and gable steps.
Surround base with a one-block-high white slab/quartz border platform offset one block from walls; ground around is lowered (excavated) in pictures.
uncertainties: Exact block type for white trim/border: looks like quartz slabs or smooth stone slabs - not certain.
Corner vertical blocks appear stone-like (could be stonebrick, andesite, or cobblestone) - identification uncertain.
Interior layout, exact window spacing in depth direction, and whether there is a ground-floor entrance are not visible; entrance location is unknown.
Precise dimensions (width/depth/height) are estimated from visible block counts in images and may be off by a few blocks.
